mitigations: Add business-impact-specific controls (approval, segregation of duties, transaction limits) given the high business/regulatory impact (Q87).
Define retention limits and deletion controls for prompts, responses, memory, logs, and vector data (Q88).
Add replay protection: nonces/idempotency keys and fresh authorization for state-changing or sensitive actions (Q91).
Introduce adversarial-prompt / jailbreak / tool-misuse regression testing (Q90).
Enforce tenant isolation and blast-radius reduction (scoped credentials, per-tenant limits) for the multi-tenant/public scale (Q86).
Use strict parsers, file validation, sandboxing, content normalization, and instruction/data isolation for the risky input formats (Q83/Q84).
Require authentication and least privilege on the public-facing surface.
Reduce exposure and add authentication, rate limiting, and security monitoring for exposed interfaces (affected: Web Chat Interface, REST API Endpoint, Third-party Integration).
Separate the system prompt from user input and treat all model input as untrusted.
Constrain model role/format and apply least privilege to any action the model can trigger.
abuse_path: External untrusted content is ingested into the RAG orchestrator.
The RAG orchestrator may use this content as part of the prompt for the LLM.
The LLM could be tricked into generating harmful or misleading outputs based on the injected prompt.
risks.control_gap: Lack of input sanitization and validation for untrusted content before it is used in LLM prompts.
llm_evidence: Edge 'edge_external_web_to_process_rag_orchestrator_untrusted_retrieved_content' allows untrusted content from external web to reach RAG orchestrator.

mitigation_actions.title: Implement strict input sanitization for user prompts
mitigation_actions.action: Add input validation and sanitization layers to all user-facing interfaces (e.g., Web Chat Interface, REST API Endpoint, Embedded Widget) to filter out malicious content such as code injection, HTML, or scripts before they are used in LLM prompts.
mitigation_actions.validation_step: Verify that all user inputs are sanitized using a whitelist-based approach and that any untrusted content is rejected or sanitized before being passed to the LLM.
maps_to_evidence: Q84: Yes, parsed and inserted with minimal validation

mitigation_actions.title: Enforce content-type restrictions for file uploads
mitigation_actions.action: Restrict file uploads to specific, safe formats (e.g., plain text, PDF, DOCX) and scan uploaded files for malicious content before processing them for LLM input.
mitigation_actions.validation_step: Confirm that file uploads are validated for content type and scanned for embedded scripts or malicious payloads before being used in LLM prompts.
maps_to_evidence: Q88: Long-term retention without clear deletion controls

identified_threats.name: Prompt injection
evidence: Edge 'edge_external_web_to_process_rag_orchestrator_untrusted_retrieved_content' allows untrusted content from external web to reach RAG orchestrator.
abuse_path: External untrusted content is ingested into the RAG orchestrator.
The RAG orchestrator may use this content as part of the prompt for the LLM.
The LLM could be tricked into generating harmful or misleading outputs based on the injected prompt.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of input sanitization and validation for untrusted content before it is used in LLM prompts.

identified_threats.name: LLM output manipulation
evidence: Edge 'edge_llm_gateway_to_entry_embedded_widget_model_output' sends model output directly to the embedded widget without validation.
abuse_path: An attacker could inject malicious content into the LLM output.
The malicious output is sent to the embedded widget, which may be displayed to users without further filtering.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of output validation and sanitization before sending LLM-generated content to end users.

identified_threats.name: Data leakage through logs
evidence: Edge 'edge_process_tool_layer_to_process_logging_monitoring_audit_log_event' sends audit logs to the logging system.
Edge 'edge_process_logging_monitoring_to_store_llm_logs_prompt_response_logs' stores logs in the LLM logs store.
abuse_path: An attacker could access the logging system and retrieve prompt/response logs.
These logs may contain sensitive information such as user data, private keys, or confidential business logic.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of encryption or access controls on logs containing sensitive information.

identified_threats.name: Untrusted data in RAG memory
evidence: Edge 'edge_process_rag_orchestrator_to_store_conversation_history_retrieval_query' sends retrieval queries to the conversation history store.
abuse_path: An attacker could inject malicious data into the conversation history store.
This data could be used by the RAG system to generate misleading or harmful responses.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of validation and sanitization for data stored in the conversation history or index training data.

identified_threats.name: Unauthorized access to internal APIs
evidence: Edge 'edge_tool_internal_api_to_business_ticketing_business_api_call' allows internal API calls to business ticketing without explicit authentication.
abuse_path: An attacker could impersonate an internal API to access business ticketing systems.
This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive user or business data.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of proper authentication and authorization checks for internal API calls.

identified_threats.name: Exposure of sensitive data through external API calls
evidence: Edge 'edge_process_tool_layer_to_external_api_direct_external_api_call' allows direct external API calls without validation.
abuse_path: An attacker could exploit the external API call to retrieve sensitive data from the system.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of validation and filtering for data sent to external APIs.

identified_threats.name: Untrusted data in external network fetches
evidence: Edge 'edge_process_api_connector_to_external_network_network_fetch' allows network fetches from external networks.
abuse_path: An attacker could inject malicious data into the network fetch request.
This data could be used to compromise the system or leak sensitive information.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of validation and filtering for data received from external networks.

identified_threats.name: Insecure data handling in user-facing responses
evidence: Edge 'edge_entry_rest_api_to_actor_authenticated_user_response' sends responses directly to authenticated users without validation.
abuse_path: An attacker could inject malicious content into the response, which is then displayed to authenticated users.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of output validation and sanitization for user-facing responses.

identified_threats.name: Exposure of internal system data through logs
evidence: Edge 'edge_process_tool_layer_to_process_logging_monitoring_audit_log_event' sends audit logs to the logging system.
abuse_path: An attacker could access the logging system and retrieve internal system data, such as user credentials or internal API keys.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of encryption or access controls on internal system logs.

identified_threats.name: Untrusted data in internal admin configuration
evidence: Edge 'edge_tool_admin_to_business_configuration_admin_configuration_change' allows admin configuration changes without validation.
abuse_path: An attacker could inject malicious configuration changes that alter system behavior or expose sensitive data.
identified_threats.control_gap: Lack of validation and filtering for admin configuration changes.

suggested_secondary_findings.name: Potential for data leakage through external API calls
evidence: Edge 'edge_process_tool_layer_to_external_api_direct_external_api_call' allows direct external API calls without validation.
suggested_secondary_findings.rationale: Further analysis is needed to determine if the external API calls are properly secured and if sensitive data is being exposed.

suggested_secondary_findings.name: Risk of unauthorized access to internal APIs
evidence: Edge 'edge_tool_internal_api_to_business_ticketing_business_api_call' allows internal API calls to business ticketing without explicit authentication.
suggested_secondary_findings.rationale: More investigation is required to assess the authentication and authorization mechanisms for internal API calls.

mitigations.title: Implement strict input sanitization for user prompts
mitigations.action: Add input validation and sanitization layers to all user-facing interfaces (e.g., Web Chat Interface, REST API Endpoint, Embedded Widget) to filter out malicious content such as code injection, HTML, or scripts before they are used in LLM prompts.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that all user inputs are sanitized using a whitelist-based approach and that any untrusted content is rejected or sanitized before being passed to the LLM.
maps_to_evidence: Q84: Yes, parsed and inserted with minimal validation

mitigations.title: Enforce content-type restrictions for file uploads
mitigations.action: Restrict file uploads to specific, safe formats (e.g., plain text, PDF, DOCX) and scan uploaded files for malicious content before processing them for LLM input.
mitigations.validation_step: Confirm that file uploads are validated for content type and scanned for embedded scripts or malicious payloads before being used in LLM prompts.
maps_to_evidence: Q88: Long-term retention without clear deletion controls

mitigations.title: Implement output validation and sanitization for LLM responses
mitigations.action: Add output validation and sanitization layers to all LLM-generated content before it is sent to end users, including filtering out potentially harmful content such as HTML, scripts, or untrusted URLs.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that all LLM outputs are sanitized using a whitelist-based approach and that any untrusted content is rejected or sanitized before being returned to the user.

mitigations.title: Enable content-type filtering for LLM responses
mitigations.action: Ensure that all LLM-generated content is marked with appropriate content types (e.g., plain text, JSON) and that any HTML or script content is explicitly blocked or escaped.
mitigations.validation_step: Confirm that all LLM outputs are marked with content types and that any HTML or script content is blocked or escaped before being rendered to the user.
maps_to_evidence: Q47: Logs contain full prompts and responses

mitigations.title: Encrypt logs containing sensitive information
mitigations.action: Implement encryption for all logs that contain sensitive information, such as LLM prompts, responses, or user data, both at rest and in transit.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that logs are encrypted using strong encryption algorithms and that access to these logs is restricted to authorized personnel only.

mitigations.title: Implement access controls for log storage
mitigations.action: Enforce strict access controls on log storage systems to ensure that only authorized users or services can access or modify logs containing sensitive information.
mitigations.validation_step: Confirm that access controls are in place and that only authorized users or services can access or modify logs containing sensitive information.

mitigations.title: Validate and sanitize data in RAG memory
mitigations.action: Implement strict validation and sanitization for all data stored in the conversation history and index training data. Use regex, schema validation, and content filtering to prevent injection or malicious payloads.
mitigations.validation_step: Audit the RAG Orchestrator code for input validation and sanitize all user-provided data before storing it in the conversation history or index training data.

mitigations.title: Secure internal knowledge base storage
mitigations.action: Ensure the internal knowledge base is stored in an encrypted, access-controlled environment. Apply role-based access controls (RBAC) to restrict access to authorized users only.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that the internal knowledge base is encrypted at rest and in transit, and that access is restricted to authenticated users with appropriate permissions.

mitigations.title: Enforce authentication and authorization for internal APIs
mitigations.action: Implement OAuth 2.0 or JWT-based authentication for all internal API calls. Enforce role-based access controls (RBAC) to ensure only authorized users can invoke specific endpoints.
mitigations.validation_step: Audit the REST API endpoint for authentication and authorization checks, and verify that all internal API calls are validated before processing.

mitigations.title: Sanitize and validate API response content
mitigations.action: Sanitize all content returned by internal APIs to prevent XSS or injection attacks. Validate the format and structure of API responses to ensure they are safe for downstream systems.
mitigations.validation_step: Inspect the Web Chat Interface for content sanitization and validate that all API responses are properly formatted and safe for rendering.

mitigations.title: Filter and validate data before external API calls
mitigations.action: Implement strict filtering and validation for all data sent to external APIs. Use schema validation and content sanitization to prevent sensitive data exposure or injection attacks.
mitigations.validation_step: Audit the Tool / Action Layer for data validation and filtering before external API calls, and ensure that all data is sanitized before transmission.

mitigations.title: Use secure communication for external API calls
mitigations.action: Ensure all external API calls are made over HTTPS with proper certificate validation. Disable insecure protocols like HTTP and ensure that data is encrypted in transit.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that all external API calls are made over HTTPS and that certificate validation is enforced for secure communication.

mitigations.title: Validate and sanitize external network data
mitigations.action: Implement strict validation and filtering mechanisms for all data received from external network sources to prevent injection or malicious payloads.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that all incoming data from external network sources is validated against a predefined schema and sanitized before processing.

mitigations.title: Sanitize user-facing response content
mitigations.action: Implement output validation and sanitization for all user-facing responses to prevent data leakage or injection attacks.
mitigations.validation_step: Check that all responses are sanitized and validated against a set of rules to ensure they do not contain malicious content.

mitigations.title: Encrypt and restrict access to internal logs
mitigations.action: Implement encryption for internal system logs and enforce access controls to ensure only authorized users can access them.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that logs are encrypted at rest and in transit, and that access controls are enforced to prevent unauthorized access.

mitigations.title: Validate and Filter Admin Configuration Inputs
mitigations.action: Implement strict validation and filtering mechanisms for all admin configuration changes to ensure only authorized and safe inputs are accepted.
mitigations.validation_step: Verify that all admin configuration inputs are validated against a predefined schema and that only authorized users can modify configurations.
maps_to_evidence: Q78: Yes, with weak or unclear limits

mitigations.title: Enforce Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for Admin Panel
mitigations.action: Implement role-based access control to restrict configuration changes to only authorized personnel, such as administrators or operators.
mitigations.validation_step: Confirm that RBAC is enforced and that only users with the appropriate roles can access or modify configuration settings.
maps_to_evidence: Q78: Yes, with weak or unclear limits

maps_to_evidence: Q78: Yes, with weak or unclear limits

quick_wins: Isolate the system prompt from user-supplied content.
Redact PII/secrets from prompts, responses, and logs.
Pin model/dependency versions and verify checksums.
Quarantine and review user-supplied content before indexing.
Validate/encode model output before rendering or executing it.
Require human approval for state-changing actions.
Move secrets and authorization out of the system prompt.
Apply per-user access control to the vector store.
